title: Atikum

infobox.value: or **Uamué**, are an [indigenous people of Brazil](/wiki/Indigenous_people_of_Brazil "Indigenous people of Brazil") that live in [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia") and [Pernambuco](/wiki/Pernambuco "Pernambuco").[[2]](#cite_note-ethno-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indigenous people of Brazil

For the language, see [Atikum language](/wiki/Atikum_language "Atikum language").

Ethnic group

Atikum

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Map of Atikum territory in [Carnaubeira da Penha](/wiki/Carnaubeira_da_Penha "Carnaubeira da Penha"), eastern Brazil | |
| Total population | |
| 7,929 (2012)[[1]](#cite_note-pib-1) | |
| Regions with significant populations | |
| [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") ( [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia"),  [Pará](/wiki/Par%C3%A1 "Pará")) | |
| Languages | |
| [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"), historically [Atikum](/wiki/Atikum_language "Atikum language")[[2]](#cite_note-ethno-2) | |
| Religion | |
| Traditional tribal religion | |

The **Atikum**, also known as **Huamuê** or **Uamué**, are an [indigenous people of Brazil](/wiki/Indigenous_people_of_Brazil "Indigenous people of Brazil") that live in [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia") and [Pernambuco](/wiki/Pernambuco "Pernambuco").[[2]](#cite_note-ethno-2)

## Territory

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atikum&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Territory")]

They have 20 villages within the Atikum Indigenous Land, and their territory is near [Carnaubeira da Penha](/wiki/Carnaubeira_da_Penha "Carnaubeira da Penha").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atikum&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Known as the "civilized Indians of the Umã Hills," the Arikum sought federal recognition from the Brazilian government beginning in the 1940s.[[1]](#cite_note-pib-1)

## Language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atikum&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Language")]

Today Atikum people speak [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"). Formerly they spoke the [Atikum language](/wiki/Atikum_language "Atikum language"), a [linguistic isolate](/wiki/Linguistic_isolate "Linguistic isolate").[[2]](#cite_note-ethno-2)
